heading: Definitions and special rules
label: 4946 Definitions and special rules

children.label: A
children.text: a substantial contributor to the foundation,

children.label: B
children.text: a foundation manager (within the meaning of subsection (b)(1)),

children.label: i
children.text: the total combined voting power of a corporation,

children.label: ii
children.text: the profits interest of a partnership, or

children.label: iii
children.text: the beneficial interest of a trust or unincorporated enterprise,

children.label: D
children.text: a member of the family (as defined in subsection (d)) of any individual described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C),

children.label: E
children.text: a corporation of which persons described in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), or (D) own more than 35 percent of the total combined voting power,

children.label: F
children.text: a partnership in which persons described in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), or (D) own more than 35 percent of the profits interest,

children.label: G
children.text: a trust or estate in which persons described in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), or (D) hold more than 35 percent of the beneficial interest,

children.label: i
children.text: which is effectively controlled (directly or indirectly) by the same person or persons who control the private foundation in question, or

children.label: ii
children.text: substantially all of the contributions to which were made (directly or indirectly) by the same person or persons described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C), or members of their families (within the meaning of subsection (d)), who made (directly or indirectly) substantially all of the contributions to the private foundation in question, and

children.label: I
children.text: only for purposes of section 4941, a government official (as defined in subsection (c)).

children.heading: Substantial contributors
children.label: 2 Substantial contributors
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “substantial contributor” means a person who is described in section 507(d)(2).

children.heading: Stockholdings
children.label: 3 Stockholdings
children.text: For purposes of paragraphs (1)(C)(i) and (1)(E), there shall be taken into account indirect stockholdings which would be taken into account under section 267(c), except that, for purposes of this paragraph, section 267(c)(4) shall be treated as providing that the members of the family of an individual are the members within the meaning of subsection (d).

children.heading: Partnerships; trusts
children.label: 4 Partnerships; trusts
children.text: For purposes of paragraphs (1)(C)(ii) and (iii), (1)(F), and (1)(G), the ownership of profits or beneficial interests shall be determined in accordance with the rules for constructive ownership of stock provided in section 267(c) (other than paragraph (3) thereof), except that section 267(c)(4) shall be treated as providing that the members of the family of an individual are the members within the meaning of subsection (d).
an officer, director, or trustee of a foundation (or an individual having powers or responsibilities similar to those of officers, directors, or trustees of the foundation), and
with respect to any act (or failure to act), the employees of the foundation having authority or responsibility with respect to such act (or failure to act).
an elective public office in the executive or legislative branch of the Government of the United States,
an office in the executive or judicial branch of the Government of the United States, appointment to which was made by the President,

children.label: A
children.text: which is listed in schedule C of rule VI of the Civil Service Rules, or

children.label: B
children.text: the compensation for which is equal to or greater than the lowest rate of basic pay for the Senior Executive Service under , United States Code,section 5382 of title 5
a position under the House of Representatives or the Senate of the United States held by an individual receiving gross compensation at an annual rate of $15,000 or more,

an elective or appointive public office in the executive, legislative, or judicial branch of the government of a State, possession of the United States, or political subdivision or other area of any of the foregoing, or of the District of Columbia, held by an individual receiving gross compensation at an annual rate of $20,000 or more,
a position as personal or executive assistant or secretary to any of the foregoing, or
a member of the Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board.

children.heading: Members of family
children.label: d Members of family
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(1), the family of any individual shall include only his spouse, ancestors, children, grandchildren, great grandchildren, and the spouses of children, grandchildren, and great grandchildren.
